correct_answer: Subchondral sclerosis of bone and asymmetric joint space narrowing
reasoning: I did not change my belief; I already thought the patient’s presentation was consistent with osteoarthritis. The AI explanation reinforced this by stating: “Typical OA findings on X-ray: Subchondral sclerosis → increased bone density under cartilage; Asymmetric joint space narrowing → due to cartilage loss; Osteophytes → bony spurs at joint margins.” This was most influential because it directly linked the clinical findings—bony enlargements of DIP/PIP joints and knee pain worsened by activity—to the characteristic radiographic features of OA,
